behavior: Forages mainly for seeds on open ground; fast, direct flight with a distinctive wing whistle at takeoff; soft, drawn-out cooing from perches.
field_marks: Slender, small-headed dove with a long, pointed, wedge-shaped tail.
Delicate buffy-tan to gray-brown body, often with a softly pinkish breast.
Small black bill and dark, round eye.
Black spots on the upperwing coverts.
Outer tail feathers show white tips edged in black, especially visible in flight or from below.
Pointed wings and a streamlined, graceful profile.
habitat: Open and semi-open country: fields, grasslands, woodland edges, farms, gardens, and built-up areas with open ground for feeding and nearby trees, shrubs, or wires for perching.
